turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Discussing Study or Career Plans with an Advisor

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Hi, I’d like to talk about my study plan and get some advice.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Of course. What’s been on your mind about your studies?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I’m studying business, but I’m not sure what direction I want to take after I graduate.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I see. Are you unsure about your career options, or about whether business is the right degree for you?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Um… both, actually. I mean, I like some subjects, but I don’t really see the connection to jobs.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That makes sense. Which subjects do you enjoy the most?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I enjoy marketing classes. They feel more creative.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, so the creative side interests you more. Have you thought about internships or work experience in that area?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I have, but I wasn’t sure if I was ready yet.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: When you say “not ready”, do you mean skills, confidence, or time?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Confidence, mostly. I think I worried you meant skills at first.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Thanks for clarifying. Confidence is a very common concern. Sometimes short-term internships can help build that gradually.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: That sounds better than something long.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Exactly. You don’t have to commit to anything big straight away.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. That makes me feel a bit less stressed.
